articles.title: T-Mobile stock (TMUS) drops into MLK Day break as investors eye fee change and Feb. 11 update
articles.summary: T-Mobile's (TMUS) stock dropped by 2.3% to $186.32 ahead of the MLK Day break, as investors anticipate an upcoming fee increase and the company's Q4 and full-year 2025 results announcement on February 11. The company plans to provide updated financial targets for 2026 and 2027 and recently completed a $2 billion senior notes offering to refinance existing debt. Analysts will be closely watching the market reaction to the fee changes and the financial update.

articles.title: The Truth About Mettler-Toledo: Why Big Money Loves It (But TikTok Barely Knows It Exists)
articles.summary: This article examines Mettler-Toledo, a company that specializes in precision measurement for various industries like pharma, food, and industrial manufacturing. While not a flashy consumer brand, it is highly valued by long-term investors for its critical role in industries requiring extreme accuracy. The article discusses why the company, despite its high stock price and premium valuation, is considered a "cop" for fundamentals-focused investors due to its essential technology and recurring revenue model, but a "flop" for those seeking viral hype or quick gains.

articles.title: The Travelers’ Benchmark: How a Record-Breaking $8.96 EPS Blowout Reshaped the Insurance Landscape
articles.summary: The Travelers Companies Inc. (NYSE: TRV) set a new industry benchmark with a record-breaking $8.96 EPS, significantly surpassing analyst expectations and showcasing impressive underwriting and investment synergy. This performance has reshaped the insurance landscape, highlighting the divide between strong performers and those facing legacy issues, while signaling the growing impact of AI, social inflation, and macro shifts like potential corporate tax cuts and tariffs. As the 2026 earnings season begins, the industry watches how Travelers and its peers will adapt to market changes, focusing on underwriting mastery over pricing power.

articles.title: Vistra Corp. Stock Is On Fire: Can This Powerhouse Keep Shockingly Outperforming The Market?
articles.summary: Vistra Corp. has seen a remarkable surge in its stock price, outperforming the market with a nearly 200% gain over the past year, driven by its critical role in the energy transition and the booming demand for electricity from AI data centers. Analysts have largely endorsed Vistra, raising price targets and re-rating the company from a traditional utility to a key player in the structural electricity shortage. The company's future performance will depend on its execution of capacity commitments, success in securing new long-term contracts with major tech and industrial customers, and favorable policy and regulatory developments, particularly concerning nuclear energy.

articles.title: Glass Lewis Recommends New Gold Shareholders Vote "FOR" the Plan of Arrangement with Coeur Mining
articles.summary: Glass Lewis, a leading independent proxy advisory firm, has recommended that New Gold shareholders vote "FOR" the proposed plan of arrangement with Coeur Mining. This recommendation supports the acquisition of New Gold by Coeur Mining, following a similar "FOR" recommendation from Institutional Shareholder Services Inc. issued earlier in January. The Special Meeting for New Gold shareholders to vote on the Transaction is scheduled for January 27, 2026.

articles.title: Upcoming Earnings Season: P&G and Capital One Face Challenges
articles.summary: The upcoming earnings season will feature reports from major companies like P&G and Capital One. P&G's revenue is estimated at $22.31 billion, affected by a government shutdown. Capital One faces pressure on the credit card industry, though talks about a new credit card product could ease concerns. Investors should also monitor the core PCE price index and management commentary during earnings calls for relevant market insights.

articles.title: Quanta Services (PWR) Valuation Check After New Grid Contracts And Record Backlog Fuel Investor Interest
articles.summary: Quanta Services (PWR) is gaining investor attention due to new energy transition contracts and a record backlog, driving its stock performance. Despite a widely followed narrative suggesting it's 1.6% undervalued at $474 per share, the company's P/E ratio of 68.4x significantly exceeds industry averages, indicating potential valuation risk if market sentiment shifts or earnings disappoint.

articles.title: Sysco Stock in Focus: Defensive Giant at a Crossroads as Foodservice Demand Softens
articles.summary: Sysco's stock has underperformed over the past year despite the company's revenue growth, cost efficiencies, and steady free cash flow, leading to investor uncertainty. While Wall Street analysts maintain a "Buy" to "Outperform" consensus with a modest price target upside, the market is waiting for clearer signs of margin improvement and traffic growth. The company's future performance hinges on managing food inflation, leveraging efficiency initiatives, and navigating shifting consumer demand in the foodservice sector.

articles.title: Goldman Sachs Q4 2025 Earnings Analysis
articles.summary: Goldman Sachs (NYSE: GS) reported a strong fourth-quarter performance for 2025, driven by a 41% surge in advisory fees within its Global Banking & Markets segment. This signals a recovery in dealmaking and positions Goldman Sachs as a key beneficiary of renewed corporate consolidation and defensive M&A strategies focusing on resilience and supply chain "friendshoring." The firm's strategic pivot back to core institutional strengths, combined with a more favorable regulatory environment, points to a strong outlook for 2026, with Goldman raising its dividend and maintaining significant share buyback capacity.

articles.title: A Look Into Kenvue Inc's Price Over Earnings
articles.summary: Kenvue Inc. (NYSE: KVUE) experienced a 0.06% share price drop, currently trading at $17.19. While the stock has seen a 1.06% increase over the past month, its one-year performance shows a 17.31% decrease. The article examines Kenvue's Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio of 22.93, which is significantly lower than the Personal Products industry average of 56.89, suggesting potential undervaluation or differing growth expectations compared to its peers.

articles.title: Assessing Prosperity Bancshares (PB) Valuation After Recent Share Price Momentum And Mixed Return Profile
articles.summary: Prosperity Bancshares (PB) is attracting investor attention due to recent share price momentum and a mixed return profile. While the stock has seen a 14.11% gain in the last 90 days, its 1-year return is a modest 5.08%. Simply Wall St's analysis indicates that PB might be undervalued with a fair value estimate of $79.50 compared to its current trading price of $72.39, driven by expected improvements in net interest margin.

articles.title: McDonald’s stock pauses at $307 as MLK Day shuts U.S. markets — what could move MCD next
articles.summary: McDonald's stock closed slightly lower at $307.43 on Friday, ahead of the MLK Day market closure. The article highlights two key factors that could influence MCD's movement next week: President Trump's newly announced tariffs on European imports, which could impact consumer spending and market sentiment, and McDonald's upcoming earnings report, expected around February 9th, where investors will focus on comparable sales and cost remarks.

articles.title: K-Wave Media risks Nasdaq delisting as shares slide, pursues bitcoin-funded turnaround - CHOSUNBIZ
articles.summary: K-Wave Media is facing potential delisting from Nasdaq due to a significant slide in its stock price. In response, the company is reportedly pursuing a turnaround strategy that involves funding through Bitcoin. This move highlights a company's attempt to innovate its financial strategy amidst market challenges.

articles.title: Sify Technologies Limited (NASDAQ:SIFY) Sees Significant Increase in Short Interest
articles.summary: Sify Technologies (NASDAQ:SIFY) experienced a significant 19.4% increase in short interest as of December 31st, totaling 553,966 shares, representing 3.3% of the company's stock sold short. This rise in short interest comes as institutional investors increase their stakes, most notably Theleme Partners LLP with a new $13.627 million position. Despite this, the company reported a loss of $0.05 EPS and negative net margin, leading analysts to carry an average "Sell" rating for the stock.

articles.title: Polaris RZR Pro R Rally Secures Third Consecutive Dakar Victory, Finishing Over 4 Hours Faster
articles.summary: The Polaris RZR Factory Racing team achieved its third consecutive Dakar Rally victory in the SSV class with their new RZR Pro R Rally, completing the race over four hours faster than the previous year's winner. Drivers Brock Heger and Max Eddy Jr. secured six stage victories, highlighting the vehicle's precision engineering built to FIA regulations and the strong collaboration with LOEB FrayMédia Motorsport. The article also includes analyst views on Polaris Inc. (PII) stock, with varied price targets and ratings.

articles.title: The Energy Sector Is Surging. Here's 1 Stock Every Investor Should Have on Their Radar.
articles.summary: GE Vernova (NYSE: GEV), a spin-off from General Electric, is highlighted as a key player in powering the surging artificial intelligence and industrial sectors. The company's power and electrification businesses are experiencing significant growth, with strong order backlogs and revenue predictions, despite a downsizing in its wind segment. GE Vernova is positioned to modernize aging power grids and has seen its stock skyrocket since its March 2024 spinoff, with increased dividends.

articles.title: Synopsys Inc Is Powering the AI Boom – But Is SNPS Stock Still Worth the Hype?
articles.summary: Synopsys Inc. (SNPS) is highlighted as a critical "picks-and-shovels" player in the AI boom, providing the essential software and IP for designing chips, distinguishing it from direct chip manufacturers. The article discusses its strong market position in Electronic Design Automation (EDA), the benefits of its IP offerings, and acknowledges its premium valuation due to growth and dominance in the AI infrastructure. It concludes that Synopsys is a solid long-term investment for those seeking AI exposure without betting on specific chip brands, despite its high price.

articles.title: Barclays Downgrades HP Inc. (HPQ) to Underweight, Cuts Target to $18
articles.summary: Barclays has downgraded HP Inc. (HPQ) to “Underweight” from “Equal Weight” and reduced its price target to $18 from $24. The firm cited "secular challenges" in HP's PC and printing businesses and a lack of catalysts, expecting the stock to remain pressured in 2026. Despite Wall Street analysts having a consensus "Buy" rating on HPQ with an average price target of $25, Barclays foresees continued pressure on the stock, especially due to the current memory cycle impacting HP's consumer-focused PC business.

articles.title: Baxter International Stock: Quiet Turnaround Play Or Value Trap In Healthcare Tech?
articles.summary: Baxter International is being re-evaluated by investors as it emerges from a restructuring phase and a spin-off of its kidney care business. The company is now focused on medical products and hospital technology, and while its stock performance has been modest, analysts see potential for a turnaround driven by operational cleanup, cost discipline, and strategic focus on higher-margin medical solutions. The next few quarters are considered crucial for management to demonstrate sustained execution and translate the strategic reset into tangible earnings growth.

articles.title: Bargains Abound in This Energy ETF
articles.summary: Amid rising oil prices and geopolitical tensions, the energy sector is experiencing significant gains. The Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight Energy ETF (RSPG) is highlighted as a fund providing access to attractively valued traditional energy stocks, with holdings like Chevron (CVX) and Devon Energy (DVN) identified as undervalued by Morningstar for their strategic positioning and capital management. The article suggests that despite impressive multi-year performance, the ETF still offers credible value opportunities.

articles.title: Down 55%, Should You Buy the Dip on Pfizer?
articles.summary: Pfizer's stock has plunged 55% from its pandemic highs due to a key drug failure and impending patent cliffs; however, it has a history as an industry survivor. Despite short-term challenges and competition from companies like Eli Lilly in the GLP-1 weight-loss drug space, Pfizer is strategically acquiring new assets and is poised for a potential long-term turnaround. Aggressive contrarian investors might find Pfizer's current low valuation and 6.6% dividend yield an attractive, albeit risky, opportunity.

articles.title: (NOA.DB.B) Investment Report (NOA.DB.B:CA)
articles.summary: This investment report for North American Construction Group Ltd. 5.50% convertible unsecured subordinated debentures (NOA.DB.B:CA) provides trading plans with buy and short targets, along with stop loss recommendations. It also presents AI-generated ratings, which are currently "Weak" across near, mid, and long terms. The article includes a list of numerous recent related articles offering diverse investment insights for the security.

articles.title: Starlink, New Leadership And Boeing Widebody Jets: What You Need To Know Before UAL's Earnings
articles.summary: United Airlines (UAL) is preparing to release its fourth-quarter earnings, with analysts expecting an EPS between $2.93 and $3.05 and revenue around $15.37-$15.44 billion. Key developments for the airline include the appointment of Vasu Raja as a new loyalty program executive, the rollout of Starlink in-flight Wi-Fi services, and plans to base its luxurious "Elevated" Boeing 787-9 Dreamliner operations out of San Francisco for routes to Singapore and London. These strategic moves and financial projections are critical points of interest ahead of the earnings report.

articles.title: Sun Life Financial (TSE:SLF) Given a C$84.00 Price Target at Royal Bank Of Canada
articles.summary: Royal Bank of Canada has set a C$84.00 price target and a "sector perform" rating for Sun Life Financial (TSE:SLF), indicating a potential downside of 3.93% from its current trading price of C$87.44. This target is lower than the analyst consensus of C$92.11, which is based on a mix of ratings including Strong Buy, Buy, Hold, and Sell. Sun Life Financial, a major Canadian insurer, recently reported C$1.86 EPS and C$9.07 billion in revenue for its latest quarter, trading with a market capitalization of C$48.42 billion.

articles.title: Barclays Downgrades HP Inc. (HPQ) to Underweight, Cuts Target to $18
articles.summary: Barclays has downgraded HP Inc. (HPQ) to "Underweight" from "Equal Weight" and lowered its price target to $18 from $24, citing significant "secular challenges" in both its PC and printing businesses. The firm anticipates continued pressure on the stock in 2026, partly due to the current memory cycle impacting HP's consumer-focused PC segment. Despite a consensus "Buy" rating from Wall Street analysts with an average price target of $25, Barclays remains cautious.

articles.title: Novo Nordisk stock drops in Copenhagen as Wegovy pill demand gets its first real test
articles.summary: Novo Nordisk's B shares fell by 2.8% in Copenhagen due to initial investor reactions to demand for its new oral Wegovy weight-loss pill. Early prescription data shows 3,100 prescriptions in the U.S. by January 9th, but overall Wegovy franchise prescriptions dropped while competitor Eli Lilly's Zepbound climbed. Investors are closely watching for updated prescription figures and the company's full-year results on February 4th, which will include the 2026 sales outlook and updates on supply and pricing.

articles.title: Occidental (OXY) Sells OxyChem, Scotiabank Lowers PT
articles.summary: Occidental Petroleum (OXY) recently sold its OxyChem business to Berkshire Hathaway for $9.7 billion to strengthen its balance sheet and focus on its oil and gas portfolio. Following this, Scotiabank slightly lowered its price target for OXY from $47 to $46, maintaining a Sector Perform rating, as part of an update for US integrated oil and gas companies. Investors are now looking at whether market volatility will impact companies' 2026 guidance and if exploration and production firms will introduce cost-cutting measures.

articles.title: RTX stock at a fresh 52-week high: what to watch before Jan. 27 earnings
articles.summary: RTX Corp (NYSE: RTX) recently hit a new 52-week high, closing at $201.92 after seven consecutive sessions of gains. Investors are now closely watching the company's upcoming fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 earnings release on January 27, particularly focusing on guidance regarding commercial aerospace delivery schedules, defense project demand, and cash-flow forecasts. Despite strong recent performance, market jitters from potential tariffs or interest rate changes, along with high expectations, pose a risk of profit-taking for RTX.

articles.title: Here's Why You Should Hold DaVita Stock in Your Portfolio for Now
articles.summary: DaVita Inc. (DVA) is recommended as a stock to hold due to its strong business model, including its patient-centric kidney care platform and international expansion. Despite a year-to-date stock decline, the company projects significant growth and has shown a positive average earnings surprise. However, concerns about its reliance on commercial payers and potential margin compression from government reimbursement changes persist.

articles.title: Quantum Computing Stocks To Watch Now - January 19th
articles.summary: MarketBeat has identified IonQ (IONQ), D-Wave Quantum (QBTS), and Quantum Computing (QUBT) as the top quantum computing stocks experiencing high dollar trading volume as of January 19th. These companies are viewed as speculative, long-horizon investments due to the emerging nature of quantum technology. IonQ offers access to its quantum systems via major cloud platforms, D-Wave provides a quantum computer and cloud access services, and Quantum Computing Inc. specializes in integrated-photonics quantum products.

articles.title: Top Waste Management Stocks To Consider - January 19th
articles.summary: MarketBeat has identified Waste Management (WM), GFL Environmental (GFL), and Custom Truck One Source (CTOS) as the top waste management stocks to watch, based on their recent high dollar trading volume. These companies represent key segments of the waste management sector, from collection and recycling to environmental services and specialized equipment. The article highlights the stable, regulated cash flows and long-lived assets typical of this sector, while also noting the associated risks and offering a call to action for further research into WM and related stocks.

articles.title: Assurant (AIZ) Acquires RL Circular Operations to Enhance Post-P
articles.summary: Assurant (AIZ) has acquired RL Circular Operations to strengthen its post-purchase service offerings with AI-driven technology, aiming to boost sustainable practices in retail and device lifecycle management. The company, operating in the Financial Services sector with a market capitalization of $11.72 billion, demonstrates robust financial health despite some warnings like a 0 Altman Z-Score and significant insider selling. Valuations suggest the stock may be modestly overvalued, though analyst sentiment remains cautiously optimistic with high institutional ownership.

articles.title: Breakeven Is Near for Harrow, Inc. (NASDAQ:HROW)
articles.summary: Harrow, Inc. (NASDAQ: HROW), an eyecare pharmaceutical company, is projected to reach breakeven within 12 months. Analysts anticipate the company will post a final loss in 2025 before achieving a profit of US$56 million in 2026, requiring an ambitious annual growth rate of 45%. The company's high debt-to-equity ratio of over 2x is noted as a risk factor for investors.

articles.title: Royal Bank Of Canada Forecasts Strong Price Appreciation for Headwater Exploration (TSE:HWX) Stock
articles.summary: Royal Bank of Canada has increased its target price for Headwater Exploration (TSE:HWX) from C$10.00 to C$11.00, maintaining a "sector perform" rating and suggesting a potential upside of 4.66%. The company's stock recently traded near its 12-month high at C$10.51, and analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average target price of C$10.25. Headwater Exploration, an oil and gas exploration and development company, reported strong financials including a C$2.51 billion market cap, a P/E of 14.4, a net margin of approximately 35.5%, and an ROE of about 28.6%.

articles.title: Manulife Financial (TSE:MFC) PT Set at C$52.00 by Royal Bank Of Canada
articles.summary: Royal Bank Of Canada has set a C$52.00 price target and an "outperform" rating for Manulife Financial (TSE:MFC). This target implies a minimal upside from the current share price, while the consensus analyst rating for Manulife Financial remains "Buy" with an average price target of C$53.13. The article also notes recent insider selling and provides an overview of the company's financial performance and market data.

articles.title: Polestar (PSNY) lands $300M equity deal as losses grow and stock sinks 60% in 2025
articles.summary: Polestar (PSNY) has secured a $300 million equity financing deal with its two largest shareholders, as the company faces increasing losses and a significant decline in its stock price. The funding aims to support the electric vehicle manufacturer's operations despite these financial challenges and its stock performance.

articles.title: Even Though Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NYSE:NCLH) Has Lost US$1.1b Market Cap in Last 7 Days, Shareholders Are Still up 42% Over 3 Years
articles.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. (NYSE:NCLH) experienced a US$1.1 billion market cap loss in the last seven days, yet its shareholders have seen a 42% return over the past three years. Despite this long-term gain, the stock is down 19% in the last year, underperforming the broader market. The article highlights the company's return to profitability within the last three years and notable insider buying activity in the recent quarter.

articles.title: 2 Dividend Stocks to Buy and Hold For 10 Years
articles.summary: This article recommends Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) and Amgen (AMGN) as two dividend stocks suitable for a 10-year hold. Both pharmaceutical companies are projected to navigate patent cliffs and competition effectively due to strong product pipelines and new drug approvals. They also offer attractive dividend programs with consistent growth, making them appealing for long-term income-seeking investors.

articles.title: ASGN Inc stock tests investor nerves as momentum cools and Wall Street turns selective
articles.summary: ASGN Inc's stock is experiencing a consolidation phase, drifting lower recently after a solid multi-month advance, leading investors to question if this is a temporary pause or a warning sign. Wall Street analysts generally hold a "Buy to Hold" consensus, seeing moderate upside potential amidst a quiet news flow and mixed macro signals. The company's future performance hinges on enterprise IT spending, federal budgets, and management's ability to balance growth with margin protection.

articles.title: NexPoint Real Estate Finance, Inc. (NYSE:NREF) Short Interest Update
articles.summary: NexPoint Real Estate Finance (NYSE:NREF) saw a 19.6% decline in short interest to 295,925 shares by December 31st, representing 3.6% of its shares and a short-interest ratio of 4.3 days. The company recently reported Q4 2025 EPS guidance, a quarterly dividend of $0.50, and has an average "Hold" rating from analysts with a $14.50 target price. Institutional investors have adjusted their stakes, and an insider transaction involved a director selling over 11,000 shares.

articles.title: MasTec Inc Is Quietly Taking Over – But Is MTZ Stock Really Worth the Hype?
articles.summary: The article discusses MasTec Inc (MTZ), an infrastructure beast working on 5G, clean energy, and power grids, suggesting it's an under-the-radar opportunity for long-term investors. It highlights MasTec's role in essential infrastructure, its ties to mega-trends like 5G and renewables, and the importance of execution and margins for a contractor. The piece weighs whether MTZ is a "cop" (worth buying) for serious investors, distinguishing it from flashy, viral stocks.

articles.title: Safety Insurance Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:SAFT) Short Interest Update
articles.summary: Short interest in Safety Insurance Group (NASDAQ:SAFT) decreased by 19.8% in December, lowering the days-to-cover ratio to 1.1 days. The company missed its quarterly earnings expectations, reporting lower EPS and revenue than anticipated. Despite this, institutional investors have increased their holdings, and Safety Insurance Group offers a quarterly dividend with an annualized yield of 4.8%.

articles.title: Promising Hydrogen Stocks To Follow Today - January 19th
articles.summary: MarketBeat's screener has identified NuScale Power (SMR), Plug Power (PLUG), and CF Industries (CF) as the top hydrogen-related stocks to watch today, based on recent high dollar trading volume. These companies are involved in various aspects of the hydrogen value chain, from nuclear power for hydrogen production to fuel-cell systems and ammonia manufacturing. While offering exposure to the clean-energy transition, hydrogen stocks are noted for their potential volatility and risks associated with technology, policy changes, and market adoption.

articles.title: ET Stock Outpaces Its Industry in the Past Month: Time to Buy or Hold?
articles.summary: Energy Transfer LP (ET) has outperformed its industry in the past month, with its stock rallying 6.4% compared to the industry's 4.1% growth and the sector's 5.8% rally. The company benefits from fee-based contracts, an extensive pipeline network across the U.S., and ongoing expansion projects, including a significant natural gas pipeline and new processing plants expected in 2026. While the stock shows a short-term bearish trend based on SMAs and a lower return on equity compared to the industry, its diversified assets, stable earnings, and attractive valuation may make it a hold for existing investors.

articles.title: Short Interest in Hanmi Financial Corporation (NASDAQ:HAFC) Drops By 19.8%
articles.summary: Hanmi Financial Corporation (NASDAQ:HAFC) experienced a significant 19.8% drop in short interest during December, totaling 578,832 shares. This represents about 2.0% of outstanding shares, with a days-to-cover ratio of 1.4 days. Institutional ownership accounts for 88.66%, and the company recently beat EPS estimates, trading at an 11.98 P/E ratio with a 3.8% dividend yield.

articles.title: Is Richardson Electronics, Ltd.'s (NASDAQ:RELL) Stock Price Struggling As A Result Of Its Mixed Financials?
articles.summary: Richardson Electronics' stock has depreciated 3.5% over the last three months, prompting an analysis of its financials. Despite a high reinvestment rate, the company's Return on Equity (ROE) is a low 0.5%, significantly below the industry average of 11%, leading to a 19% decline in net income over five years. While analysts predict future earnings growth, the current mixed financial performance raises questions about the efficiency of its capital reinvestment and potential impact on its stock price.

articles.title: PRA Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:PRAA) Sees Significant Drop in Short Interest
articles.summary: PRA Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:PRAA) experienced a 19.6% decline in short interest in December, reaching 717,337 shares, representing about 1.9% of shares short. Analysts hold a consensus "Hold" rating with an average price target of $25.00, despite some recent downgrades and price target reductions. Insider activity showed Director Geir Olsen purchasing 15,000 shares, increasing his stake by 34.14%, while institutional investors maintain a significant ownership of 97.22%.

articles.title: Texas Pacific Land's (NYSE:TPL) 31% CAGR Outpaced the Company's Earnings Growth Over the Same Five-year Period
articles.summary: Texas Pacific Land (NYSE:TPL) saw its share price increase by 269% over five years, corresponding to a 31% compound annual growth rate (CAGR), which outpaced its 19% annual earnings per share (EPS) growth over the same period. This suggests that the market's perception of the company has become more optimistic. Despite a recent 28% decline in the last year, long-term shareholders have benefited from a 292% total shareholder return (TSR) over five years when including dividends.

articles.title: 3 REITs Every Investor Should Know About
articles.summary: This article highlights three REITs—Realty Income (O), Vici Properties (VICI), and Digital Realty Trust (DLR)—as top investment picks following recent interest rate cuts. Realty Income offers stable, monthly dividends from its diversified retail properties; Vici Properties benefits from long-term, inflation-indexed leases with experiential tenants like casinos; and Digital Realty is poised for growth in data centers due to the expansion of cloud and AI markets. The author suggests these REITs are becoming more attractive as interest rates decline and their valuations remain reasonable.

articles.title: Expeditors International Earnings Outlook: Key Points to Watch
articles.summary: Expeditors International (EXPD), a Fortune 500 logistics company, is set to announce its Q4 2025 results by the end of February, with analysts predicting a 13.1% decline in EPS to $1.46, despite the company consistently beating forecasts. The company's stock has surged 46% over the past year, outperforming major indices, driven by strong global freight demand. While most analysts maintain a "Hold" rating, the highest price target suggests potential for further appreciation.
